name: Google Cloud Prompt Engineering for Generative AI
description: Google Cloud guide to prompt engineering fundamentals. Covers direct prompting (zero-shot), one-shot, few-shot, and multi-shot techniques, chain-of-thought prompting, role prompting, prompt iteration strategies, and best practices for structuring effective LLM interactions.

name: Google Antigravity Tutorial & Documentation
description: Comprehensive tutorial for Google Antigravity, an agent-first development platform. Covers autonomous AI agent workflows, spec-driven development, multi-agent parallel execution, AGENTS.md cross-tool rules, browser subagent testing, MCP integrations, and agent skills for modular AI capabilities.

name: Proxmox Virtual Environment (VE)
description: Free, open-source Type 1 hypervisor integrating KVM and LXC containers with a web-based management interface. The most popular platform for cybersecurity home labs due to its support for VM snapshots, clustering, ZFS storage, and software-defined networking. Ideal for running isolated malware analysis environments, Active Directory labs, and full SOC stacks.

name: Oracle VirtualBox
description: Free, open-source cross-platform Type 2 hypervisor for x86/x64 and ARM hardware. Supports Windows, Linux, macOS, and Solaris guests with snapshots, shared folders, and seamless mode. Widely used for running Kali Linux, REMnux, DVWA, and other security VMs on personal machines. Includes labs, tutorials, and training resources.

name: VMware Workstation Pro (Free)
description: Industry-standard Type 2 desktop hypervisor now free for all use cases including commercial. Supports advanced networking (NAT, bridged, host-only, custom virtual networks), snapshots, clones, and integration with VMware vSphere. Commonly used for cybersecurity training labs, malware analysis sandboxes, and certification exam prep environments.

name: HashiCorp Vagrant
description: Open-source tool for building and managing portable virtual machine environments via code. Define entire lab topologies in a Vagrantfile and spin them up with a single command. Supports VirtualBox, VMware, Hyper-V, and Docker providers. Used to automate reproducible security lab provisioning -- spin up Active Directory domains, vulnerable networks, or SOC stacks on demand.

name: Blue Team Home Lab Guide
description: Curated collection of resources for building an enterprise-grade blue team home lab. Covers SOC architecture, SIEM deployment, network security monitoring, log collection pipelines, threat hunting environments, and integration of tools like Security Onion, Wazuh, ELK, and Splunk.

name: CyberDefenders – Blue Team Training
description: Free blue team training platform with hands-on labs and challenges. Practice DFIR, threat hunting, malware analysis, and network forensics using real-world scenarios. Browser-based lab environments with structured learning paths from beginner to advanced. Community leaderboard and certifications.

name: Pi-hole – Network-wide DNS Ad Blocking
description: Free, open-source DNS sinkhole for network-wide ad and tracker blocking. Runs on Raspberry Pi, Docker, or any Linux machine. Includes a web dashboard with query logging, statistics, and whitelist/blacklist management. A foundational home lab component for learning DNS filtering, network traffic analysis, and privacy-enhancing technologies.

name: MISP – Open Source Threat Intelligence Platform
description: Free, open-source threat intelligence platform for collecting, storing, distributing, and sharing indicators of compromise and threat data. Integrates with TheHive, Cortex, Suricata, and SIEM platforms. Includes MITRE ATT&CK mapping, STIX/TAXII support, 300+ galaxies and taxonomies, and community threat feeds. Essential home lab component for CTI practice.

name: DetectionLab
description: Automated lab environment with Windows Active Directory, Splunk, osquery, Sysmon, and Velociraptor for practicing detection engineering. Deploys via Vagrant/Packer to VirtualBox or VMware. Pre-configured with logging pipelines, security tools, and attack simulation capabilities for testing detection rules and incident response procedures.

name: Ludus – Automated Home Lab Builder
description: Automated cybersecurity lab deployment tool built on Proxmox. Spin up Active Directory environments, vulnerable machines, and attack infrastructure with simple YAML configuration. Supports templates for common scenarios like red team labs, malware analysis, and certification prep. Drastically reduces lab setup time from days to minutes.

name: Awesome Cyber Security Home Labs
description: Community-curated awesome list of cybersecurity home lab resources. Covers hypervisors, network simulation, vulnerable VMs, SIEM/SOC tools, Active Directory labs, cloud security labs, malware analysis environments, and automation tools. A comprehensive starting point for planning and building any type of security lab.

name: SimplyCyber Home Lab Walkthrough
description: Gerald Auger's SimplyCyber YouTube channel with home lab build walkthroughs, SOC analyst career guidance, and hands-on cybersecurity tutorials. Covers building a SOC home lab on a budget, resume building, interview prep, and practical blue team skills. One of the most recommended starting points for cybersecurity career changers.

name: Cloud Security Labs – Free Tier Environments
description: PurpleCloud: automated Azure AD lab deployment for purple team exercises using Terraform. Deploys a complete Active Directory environment in Azure with attack simulation tools, Sentinel logging, and detection engineering resources. Ideal for those who prefer cloud-based labs over on-premises hardware.
